name: Flipside Crypto Blockchain Analytics API
what_it_does: Blockchain analytics API providing SQL-queryable on-chain data across 20+ blockchains — agents run SQL queries against curated blockchain datasets covering transactions, DeFi protocols, NFTs, and addresses with pre-built table schemas.
use_cases: Querying on-chain DeFi metrics (TVL, swap volumes, liquidity) via SQL API
Analyzing wallet transaction history and token holdings across multiple chains
Building blockchain dashboards with custom SQL queries against curated tables
Researching NFT market trends using structured on-chain data
Creating automated blockchain analytics reports from agent-run SQL queries
not_for: Real-time transaction monitoring requiring <1 second latency (use Helius or QuickNode)
Private/permissioned blockchain data without public indexing
Non-SQL users — Flipside requires SQL knowledge for data queries
best_when: An agent needs to run complex analytical SQL queries against curated, normalized blockchain data across multiple chains without building indexing infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need real-time blockchain event streaming — Flipside data has ingestion latency.

auth.notes: API key from Flipside account. Passed as header. Python SDK (flipside) handles auth. Query results cached — same query returns cached results within TTL.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 100 query results/day, limited compute credits
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $49/month; Business from $199/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Credit-based pricing for query compute. Caching reduces credit consumption for repeated queries. Competitive with Dune Analytics for blockchain SQL analytics.

performance.rate_limits: Query rate limits per plan; complex queries may time out

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python SDK well-documented. REST API for query submission and result retrieval. Query execution is async — poll for completion. Error responses include SQL error details. Result caching prevents credit waste.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SQL queries are idempotent reads. Same query returns cached results (idempotent with TTL). Query submission is idempotent by query text.
known_agent_gotchas: Query execution is async — agents must poll for results (typically 5-30 seconds)
Query timeouts apply — complex queries may need optimization
Data freshness varies by chain (usually hours behind tip) — not suitable for real-time
SQL dialect is Snowflake SQL — different from standard SQL in some ways

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. No formal compliance certifications. Blockchain data is public — no PII in datasets. US data residency.
